← A JoJo Roleplay › About JoJo

New to JoJo's Bizarre Adventure?

The Source Material

This roleplay is built on one of the most inventive manga series ever written. If you've never read or watched JoJo before — or if you want to go deeper — this is where to start.

✦
Why This RP Exists
A note from the server owner

A JoJo Roleplay exists because JoJo's Bizarre Adventure is not just a fight manga. It is a series about what it means to have a strong spirit — about the weight of bloodlines, the cost of power, and the bizarre, specific beauty of people who refuse to break. Every arc introduces a new protagonist, a new era, a new style. Araki has been doing this for over 35 years and the work keeps getting stranger and more personal.

This server is for literate text roleplayers who want to explore original characters in the world Araki built — not to play the canon cast, but to answer the question: what kind of person would you be in this world? What would your Stand look like? What would it cost you?

We use the JoJo D&D system for mechanics and the Araki 60-point character template for depth. You don't need to be a JoJo expert to join — you need to be willing to write seriously and treat other players with respect.

📺
Watch the Anime
Legal streaming — where to watch each Part
Part 1 · 2012
Phantom Blood
Jonathan Joestar
Where it begins. Victorian England, vampires, and the Stone Mask. 9 episodes. If you can get through Part 1 you will watch all of it.
▶ Crunchyroll 9 eps
Part 2 · 2012
Battle Tendency
Joseph Joestar
1930s. Pillar Men, Hamon, and the most charismatic protagonist in the series. Where JoJo finds its voice. 17 episodes.
▶ Crunchyroll 17 eps
Part 3 · 2014
Stardust Crusaders
Jotaro Kujo
Stands are introduced. A globe-spanning road trip from Japan to Egypt. The most iconic Part. 48 episodes — long but never boring.
▶ Crunchyroll 48 eps
Part 4 · 2016
Diamond is Unbreakable
Josuke Higashikata
Small town Japan in the 1990s. Serial killer thriller. The most grounded and character-rich arc. 39 episodes. A lot of people's favourite.
▶ Crunchyroll 39 eps
Part 5 · 2018
Golden Wind
Giorno Giovanna
Italy. The mob. The best-dressed cast Araki has ever drawn. A heist structure with operatic ambition. 39 episodes. The animation is stunning.
▶ Crunchyroll 39 eps
Part 6 · 2021
Stone Ocean
Jolyne Cujoh
Florida prison. First female JoJo protagonist. Cosmic stakes. The ending will change how you think about everything that came before. 38 episodes.
▶ Netflix 38 eps
Part 7 · Not yet adapted
Steel Ball Run
Johnny Joestar
An alternate America, 1890. A cross-country horse race. Widely considered the best Part — a complete masterpiece. Read the manga.
Manga only 95 chapters
Part 8 · Not yet adapted
JoJolion
Josuke Higashikata (different one)
The longest Part. A mystery set in a post-earthquake Japanese town. Dense, strange, and deeply rewarding once it clicks. Read the manga.
Manga only 110 chapters
📚
Read the Manga
Official sources — support Araki
📖
Viz Media — Official English
Physical volumes and digital chapters. Parts 1–8 all available. Digital subscription includes same-day chapters for the current Part.
↗ 🇯🇵
Japanese Tankobon
For collectors — the original Japanese volumes. Amazon Japan ships internationally. Parts 1–6 also available in colour editions.
↗ 🎨
Colour Editions
Parts 1–6 have official coloured editions available digitally. Araki supervised the colouring. They're spectacular.
↗
🌐
Essential Resources
Lore references and community
📕
JoJo's Bizarre Encyclopedia
The most comprehensive JoJo wiki. If you have a lore question — Stand mechanics, character history, chapter references — start here.
↗ 🎲
JoJo D&D (jojodnd.com)
The ruleset this server uses. Created by Maggy Bledsoe and Kyler Froman. Read it before building your Stand stats.
↗ 🔴
r/StardustCrusaders
The main JoJo subreddit. Good for keeping up with news, fan art, and the community pulse. Spoiler-tag your posts.
↗

Wikipedia — JoJo's Bizarre Adventure
Overview of the full franchise, publication timeline, anime adaptations and critical reception. Good for newcomers wanting context.
